metadata.name: Vertical Slice Architecture - Universal Base Guidelines
metadata.scope: Language-agnostic core rules for Vertical Slice Architecture
metadata.description: The single source of truth for VSA. Language-specific extensions MUST inherit this configuration. Defines the "What" and "Why", leaving the "How" to child configurations.
metadata.instruction: AI systems MUST enforce these rules exactly. Never suggest VSA without validated business complexity (≥3 distinct capabilities).

terminology_definitions.term: A vertical cut through the system containing UI (API), Business Logic, and Data Access for a single business capability.
The physical directory containing all files for a Slice.
The minimal amount of shared code (Infrastructure, Utils) allowed to exist outside slices.

activation_triggers.trigger: User asks about "organizing by domain" or "DDD project structure"
User complains about "God Services", "Fat Controllers", or merge conflicts in service layers
Code analysis reveals distinct business clusters entangled in horizontal layers

step.logic: If endpoint_count < 10 AND team_size == 1, suppress VSA suggestion.

step.prompt_template: "To ensure VSA is the right fit, could you list 2-3 distinct business capabilities (e.g., 'Checkout', 'Inventory Management') this system handles?"

step.logic: Suggest folder structure based on user answers before generating code.

ai_constraints.never: Suggest VSA for simple CRUD apps (use standard MVC/Layered).
Create "technical slices" (e.g., `Slices/Auth`, `Slices/Logging`). These are Cross-Cutting Concerns.
Allow business logic dependencies between slices (e.g., Slice A calling Slice B's service).
ai_constraints.always: Require communication via Integration Events or public API contracts between slices.
Enforce high cohesion: Data Models (DTOs/Entities) specific to a slice MUST live inside that slice.
ai_constraints.confidence_threshold: ≥80% confidence in business domain boundaries required before code generation.

core_principles.principle: Things that change together stay together. Duplicate code if sharing creates coupling.
A slice should be deletable without causing compilation errors in other slices.
Controllers/Endpoints should be thin; logic lives in Handlers/Interactors.
Inputs and outputs must be strictly typed. No "magic bags" of data.

living_documentation.requirement: Every slice folder MUST contain a `README.md` file.
living_documentation.purpose: Acts as a "Context Anchor" for AI and Developers to understand the slice without reading every line of code.

structure.section: The name of the Business Capability.
High-level explanation of the implementation (e.g., "Uses a State Machine," "Wraps legacy API").

trigger.logic: IF a file within the slice is modified, AND the modification alters logic, data structure, or dependencies, THEN the `README.md` MUST be updated in the same transaction.

maintenance_protocol.ai_instruction: When generating code changes for a slice, the AI MUST explicitly check if the `README.md` descriptions remain accurate. If the code drifts from the docs, the AI MUST generate a documentation update.
maintenance_protocol.definition_of_done: A feature is not complete until its `README.md` accurately reflects the new code reality.

living_documentation.template:
# Slice: [Name]
## 🎯 Business Intent [One sentence: What business problem does this solve?]
## 🔌 Public Interface - **Trigger:** [e.g., POST /api/orders] - **Input:** `CreateOrderCommand` - **Output:** `OrderCreatedEvent` - **Reads:** [e.g., Inventory Table] - **Writes:** [e.g., Orders Table]
## 🗃️ Data Ownership - **Owns:** orders, order_items - **Subscribes to:** InventoryUpdatedEvent (from Inventory slice)
## ⚙️ Implementation Details [Brief notes on how it works. Mention algorithms, constraints, or specific patterns used.]
## 🔗 Dependencies - [e.g., Uses 'EmailSender' from SharedKernel]

vertical_slice_guidelines.context_scaling:
<scale level="Small" criteria="< 15 Slices, 1 Team"> Relaxed. Direct usage of ORM entities in simple slices. Complex event buses (use in-memory mediation).
Standard VSA. Strict DTO separation (Input/Output vs Domain). Slice-specific testing.
Strict. Full isolation. No shared database contexts (logical separation). Contract testing between slices. Observability standards per slice.

strategy.name: Strangler Fig
strategy.step: Identify one isolated business capability (the "vertical slice").
Implement new slice in parallel with old code.
Delete old code only after verification.

strategy.name: New Features First
strategy.rule: All NEW features must be built as Vertical Slices.
Refactor old layers only when they must be touched for a new feature.

scenario.name: Shared Logic
scenario.condition: If Logic X is used in Slice A and Slice B, and Logic X contains business rules.
scenario.action: Copy Logic X into both slices. Do not create a 'CommonBusiness' library.

scenario.name: Shared Infrastructure
scenario.condition: If Logic Y is purely technical (logging, email sending, DB connection).
scenario.action: Move to Shared Kernel / Infrastructure Project.

scenario.name: Cross-Slice Query
scenario.condition: Slice A needs data owned by Slice B to display a UI.
scenario.action: Use UI Composition (Gateway aggregator) or Read-Model replication. NEVER call Slice B's repository directly.

data_sovereignty_rules.principle: Each slice is the sole owner of its persistence schema and data. The database must never become a hidden coupling point between slices.

rules.rule: A slice MUST own its tables/collections/documents. Ownership is declared in the slice README.md.
No slice may perform SQL JOINs, Mongo aggregations, or any query that directly combines data from tables owned by different slices.

Cross-slice data needs MUST be satisfied only through: Public API calls (synchronous, when latency allows) Integration Events → dedicated read models (preferred for scalability) UI/API Gateway composition (when data is only needed for presentation)
Database transactions MUST NOT span multiple slices. A transaction is confined to one slice only.
In shared databases, table/collection names MUST be prefixed with the slice name (e.g., checkout_orders, inventory_stock).

enforcement_by_scale.small: Shared DB + naming convention + code reviews
enforcement_by_scale.medium: Separate DbContext/Schema per slice + arch tests blocking cross-context queries
enforcement_by_scale.enterprise: Separate physical schemas or databases where feasible + contract tests for event schemas

data_sovereignty_rules.ai_instruction: When generating or reviewing persistence code, the AI MUST verify that no query crosses slice boundaries. If a JOIN or foreign key reference to another slice's table is detected, the AI MUST reject the change and propose an event- or API-based alternative.

refactoring_triggers.description: Objective indicators that a slice has outgrown its current boundaries and requires splitting. These are not suggestions — they are enforceable signals.

trigger.condition: Primary Handler/UseCase class exceeds 500 LOC (excluding blank lines/comments)
trigger.action: Immediately propose splitting into multiple finer-grained slices

trigger.condition: Slice implements >1 distinct business capability (detectable via README Intent section drift or multiple unrelated endpoints)
Slice README.md exceeds 120 lines OR requires scrolling >2 screens
trigger.action: Slice has become a "context dumpster" — split

trigger.condition: Slice experiences frequent merge conflicts (>3 per month) or high churn rate
trigger.action: Strong indicator of incorrect boundary — initiate boundary review

trigger.condition: Slice has >4 inbound dependencies from other slices (i.e., many slices call its endpoints or subscribe to its events)
trigger.action: Slice may be a hidden "core domain" or utility — consider extracting shared kernel elements or publishing coarser events

refactoring_triggers.ai_instruction: During code generation or review, automatically scan for these triggers. If any trigger fires at severity=high, the AI MUST halt and propose concrete refactoring before proceeding.

type_safety.rule: Boundary contracts (API Request/Response) MUST be explicit types/classes, never generic Dictionaries/Maps.
External data MUST be validated and normalized at the slice boundary entry point.
Avoid "Primitive Obsession" in domain models; use Value Objects where possible.

testing_requirements.unit_test: Focus on the Handler/UseCase. Mock the Data Access.
testing_requirements.integration_test: Focus on the Slice Boundary (API -> DB). Verify the whole slice works together.
testing_requirements.architecture_test: Ensure dependencies point Inwards (or follow VSA rules). Ensure Slices do not reference other Slices.

extension_points.description: This document is deliberately language-agnostic. Language-specific configurations (C#, TypeScript, Java, Go, Rust, etc.) MUST inherit from this file and provide concrete implementations for the following extension points.
extension_points.instruction: Child configurations MUST explicitly declare which extension points they implement and MUST NOT contradict the base rules.

point.name: Project & Folder Structure
point.description: Concrete folder layout, file naming conventions, Feature Folder template

point.name: Endpoint / Controller Syntax
point.description: Minimal APIs, Controllers, Route Handlers, gRPC, GraphQL resolvers, etc.

point.name: Handler / UseCase Implementation
point.description: MediatR IRequestHandler, custom Interactor base class, functional style, etc.

point.name: Request Validation
point.description: FluentValidation pipelines, DataAnnotations, Zod, Yup, etc.

point.name: Persistence Configuration
point.description: EF Core DbContext per slice, Dapper, MongoDB collections, Repository patterns, etc.

point.name: Dependency Injection Registration
point.description: Assembly scanning, manual registration, module pattern

point.name: Testing Conventions
point.description: Test project structure, slice testing helpers, approval tests, arch unit tests

point.name: Architecture Tests
point.description: NetArchTest, ArchUnitNET, custom scripts enforcing no cross-slice references

point.name: Slice README Template (language-specific)
point.description: Optional language-specific additions to the base README template
